review_title: Good versatile software
author: Ronald C.
pros: Loved using this software, although it was only a trial version. Easy to use and versatile software for ministry in any size congregation.
cons: We could not keep this in the budget and that is why we could only use the trial version. We didn't have any problems.

review_title: Amazing Support for a Great Price
description: Overall I have had a great experience using Ekklesia 360. I had great support during the building and implementing phase. Now, when I get stuck, I can almost always find what I need in the Help section, which is very detailed with lots of instructions. If I can't find it, customer support has always been fantastic and has helped me make little fixes I couldn't figure out. I feel confident maintaining and enhancing my website with Ekklesia 360.
author: Jacquelyn V.
pros: The customer service has been amazing. Any issue I have had has been resolved / fixed / explained and I am always grateful!
cons: Building complex pages can be confusing - there are numerous components that can get tangled up - monklets, sections, responsive columns. It just takes a lot of patience and a sharp eye in the code, but it can all be done.

review_title: Outdated platform for serious church websites.
description: We’ve been using Ekklesia360 for our church website, and while the front-end appearance is clean and mobile-friendly, the back-end experience leaves a lot to be desired. The editing interface is very limiting and unintuitive. The WYSIWYG editor does not accurately reflect what the finished page will look like, which makes layout adjustments frustrating and time-consuming. Many of the help guides and setup documentation do not match the current platform, which adds confusion when trying to troubleshoot. Support is handled through a ticket system, and response times can be slow, especially when dealing with time-sensitive updates. The media system is especially restrictive. There’s no way to directly attach sermon podcasts to third-party platforms outside of the one they support. Integration with Apple Podcasts or Spotify isn’t straightforward, and there’s no filtering system for media items. If you run multiple podcasts, there isn’t a clean or direct way to manage or display them separately. On the positive side, the site does look good on mobile devices, and integration with ShelbyNext’s resource calendar works well. Overall, it functions, but the platform feels dated and overly restrictive for churches that need flexibility in media, podcasting, and content management.
author: Kyle M.
pros: It looks nice on mobile. Integrates well with ShelbyNext resource calendar. That's all I can say that's positive about it.
cons: The backend system is an absolute train wreck. And not having direct access to certain "template driven" elements means spending days or even weeks waiting for tech support to respond with assistance.

review_title: Wonderful product that continues to grow with us!
description: A very effective , up-to-date and nice looking website.
author: Kathy H.
pros: Ekklesia 360 is a great website development product. The flexibility in design options allows me to be creative when I update the site, but the staff of Ekklesia 360 is also readily available to help me make changes, explain how to do things better and also to make the changes for us at no charge or for a reasonable fee. The staff at Ekklesia has been a pleasure to deal with in all sorts of situations. I have worked extensively with various staff members as we have significantly updated our website twice.
cons: I would love to be able to make some of the changes myself, but there are some things that can only be done by an Ekklesia employee. I understand that these precautions are in place for a reason, but I have run across a few things that it would have been quicker and easier if I could have just been allowed to make the changes on my own.

review_title: Best Church Hosting
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We have loved our experience with ekklesia, we have gone with 3 total website designs with them. They are a very cost effective solution to easy and great looking websites. The customer support has always went of the way to fix or help in any way they can.
cons: Can be pricey to setup sites and custom work is generally pretty pricey to have done. Knowing its for the "non-profit" market it surprises me that there isn't more flexibility on pricing.

review_title: They Promise The Moon Then Disappear
description: Again, it looks awesome until you need help. It's not as intuitive as they make it out to be.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Everything that they said it could do. Things like automated payment collection and basic CRM functionality.
cons: This has been such a frustrating experience. During our process for selecting a new product we specifically said we care most about customer care. We were promised a dedicated account manager and full-time support availability. Once they made the sale it was like pulling teeth getting help.

review_title: Robust 'Communication Management System'
description: This is a good solid platform which met our requirements and has scaled well for our 2000+ member church with 30+ content contributors. We were able to consolidate 5 web sites and are consolidating over 10 different e-mail management processes on to one platform. You need to be willing to dive in and 'learn it' with hands on experience as the documentation is a bit thin. Product is well supported and vendor is continuing to actively develop and improve the product . We've been pleased with the product and recommend it for other churches.
author: Matson S.
pros: Solid SaaS tool for church management of web sites, group e-mail, and event registration. System functionality is well segmented which allows them to offer a robust multi-level security / user permissions management. Multimedia management support is fairly solid for many media types. Very flexible web presentation layer and can be designed to have very custom look and feel. Event management (calendaring and invitation / registration) has worked well. Implementing payment processing function and working well so far. We haven't found any major defects with the platform.
cons: The HTML editor is OK for basic page creation and it does allow for direct editing of HMTL code - however - application does require end users to have some basic knowledge of page design and asthetics. User security administration can be maddening, difficult to track down the 'why does this work for him but not her' scenarios. Administration interface is a bit clunky not always consistent in behavior from module to module, but is not defective. Customer support has always been helpful, but they are stretched thin Platform does require solid technical resources to help a church implement it, mainly for internal project management, training, and new operational process.

review_title: LOVE LOVE LOVE Ekklesia. Such a great product and company. Easy to use and learn.
author: John S.
pros: It is very easy to use and provides great templates for getting started. Their customer support team is incredible, they seem to care about your website and the purpose of the organization. The software is very affordable and had packages that are easily adjustable for any budget.
cons: The software sometimes is slow to load, which can be frustrating. Though it is easy to learn, there are some skills and options that required more reading and trial and error to learn. The training videos are lacking... although you can schedule a training and ask questions, but I'd like the video library to be larger.

review_title: Not worth your money
description: In the 2+ years I've been with this organization, I've had nothing but problems with Ekklesia and can't figure out why someone chose this route in the first place. Stay clear.
author: Adam J.
pros: Not much. I don't really have much positive to say besides the fact that it does put a website on the web. That's about it.
cons: Constant bugs that don't get addressed, horrible UI, bad response time from customer service, confusing button mapping, and expensive (when compared to easier to use companies like Wix). This is definitely something I would steer clear of. If someone designed a UI that looks like it dates back to 2003 and randomly selected how features and button layout would look, this would be the software. Also, our organization originally asked them to design our site (and spent tens of thousands of dollar on design) and it looks horrible (this decision was made before I joined this organization). It's a shame we wasted so much money on a poorly designed website.

review_title: Excellent and Powerful Web Site Builder
description: My overall experience has been very positive. I am not technically advanced with Web sites, but I am able to make changes and additions to our Web site each week with little to no hassle. When I have an issue or a need, the technical support team is on it by the next day at the very latest.
author: Daniel E.
pros: Ekklesia 360 is a Web site builder that is incredibly easy to use, intuitive, feature-rich, and has an incredibly attentive and results-oriented customer service team.
cons: I wish Ekklesia 360 had more design choices; however, this is not to say they lack options. Also, I wish they had a one-stop-shop for live streaming.

review_title: Long time user, very disappointed with recent customer service experience
description: I've been with Ekklesia for 7 years now and overall things have been good. This past fall I had a login issue pop up that needed someone on their team to fix for me. I had to sent multiple emails and make multiple calls. In the end, the fix took about 5 minutes (which I knew was the case) but their customer service was awful. I asked to speak with someone in management, was told I would be contacted and have still heard nothing. I've waited a month to post this review hoping to get this matter resolved. I will no longer recommend Ekklesia to colleagues how ask me about my experience.
author: Matt M.
pros: I liked this platform because of the design templates and ease of use from a user perspective. The platform also integrates well with other digital tools we utilize.
cons: Nothing big here, it's reasonable for the price-point.

review_title: Ekklesia CMS a good fit for us
description: Helped us easily update our digital brand and gave us the tools to be more effective in telling our story.
author: Sonja P.
pros: Offers an array of templates for church websites, making it easy to create or redesign your site. Support was great through the process. They made migration of content really smooth.
cons: Some limits on font styles for headers but not a huge issue. I think they would accommodate a need.

review_title: Good web software for newbies
description: We use this software to update our website. I love that it translates the code in the background by giving me simple, plug-n-play instructions.
author: Cheri C.
pros: The software makes it easy for anyone to update a website without any headaches.
cons: It does cost a few dollars to work with this software.

review_title: I like the intuitiveness of the product. It is easy to create, functionality, and navigation.
author: Jean K.
pros: The ease in which pages are created. The website helps support our mission to the community and to the church congregation. Look and functionality are things that people have stated they like.
cons: It can be limiting at times. Some modules don't always allow for the customization that we would like to have and present on the website.

review_title: The easy to use website builder
description: Overall, Ekklesia is ideal for those companies who are in need of a website without the hassle of creating one from scratch. With very easy to use templates that are modern in style and efficient ways to integrated other programs, it’s highly usable and most recommended.
author: Evangelia M.
pros: What I love most about Ekklesia 360 was how easy it was to use, the templates provided, as well as incorporating other programs.
cons: An area of improvement for Ekklesia 360 would possibly be to have more template options that are more diversely designed

review_title: Riverstone Church
description: Great company and great product. I recommend it to churches, helps to have some experience with coding.
author: Tracy W.
pros: Customer support very helpful and prompt
cons: Site became a bit outdated, could get confusing making updates

review_title: Good stuff
author: Doug D.
pros: I have enjoyed setting up our website on your platform. The representative that helped me was superb in guiding me and making sure I was on track and on time
cons: I don't get timely reponses now from customer service. It seems to have slowed down since I moved on from the building phase

review_title: Ekklesia 360 is a lackluster web design platform
description: Ekklesia 360 is unnecessarily difficult to use and outdated by platforms like Squarespace.
author: Patrick T.
pros: Ekklesia 360 provides many options when uploading sermons. You can choose from many different criteria which help users find your content easily.
cons: Ekklesia 360 is very difficult to navigate. The way pages are put together is not logical. This makes quickly changing content difficult and locating an exact page a journey.

review_title: Pretty Good, but more expensive than standard website builder
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like that it's focused on churches so it's made with the idea that you'll want a place to upload audios or videos of your Sunday Message.
cons: It's more expensive than other website builders and not as user friendly--which makes sense since companies like squarespace are targetting a larger market and probably have a larger budget.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Website Design
description: We have had great response from the general public about the ease of surfing our site. We enjoyed working with the team and our site was up on time.
author: Jeff R.
pros: We really enjoy our new website. It is easy to update and looks new and relevant. The team at Ekklesia 360 is outstanding. Great product and support team.
cons: It had a bit of a learning curve to get used to it. Once we dove in it came easier.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Set aside some time to learn this product. Not happy with it at all
author: Robbey S.
pros: The result can be fantastic with enough work, study and tech support. In comparison to other products, it's not worth the investment of time.
cons: Huge learning curve, the ability to lose all your data without following a few steps is a real problem. If you want to put in hours of learning, then this software is for you.

review_title: Professional and Quality
description: We just switched to Ekklesia 360 from another web host. We love the simple to use web templates. The support department is top notch. They take the time to not only share the "how to" steps, but even include little video helps that guide you through the settings and fixes that you were looking for. I highly recommend this company!
author: Andrew P.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Ekklesia is fine
description: Ekklesia is good for church websites, as long as you don't mind not having much control over the layout. A lot of it is not that customizable, and it isn't all that user-friendly.
author: Bethany H.
pros: They got it up and going for us.
cons: Now that it's up & going, we can't easily customize our webpages.

review_title: I appreciate the ease of this new database. Much more user friendly!!
author: Stephanie C.
pros: I appreciate the new navigation system more than when this first came out. It's much easier to use. I primarily add people to the database and really like the ease of how easy it is to do this now. Thank you for the update!!
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: This is the program that my work has changed to for updating the website for our church.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I actually am having a hard time learning this program. I am the person who updates the children's portion of the website.
cons: It is not real user friendly unless you are tech savvy. I don't know a lot about how to publish data on the site and find it hard to learn.
